name: Nevada Speech-Language Pathologist License
description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Nevada - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Board of Examiners for Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology
provider.parentOrganization: Nevada Board of Examiners for Audiology and SLP

address.streetAddress: 5011 Meadowood Mall Way, Suite 300, Reno, NV 89502
address.description: 2270 South Jones Blvd, Suite 120, Las Vegas, NV 89146

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Nevada SLP license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: $300 initial. $200 biennial renewal. Nevada has among the highest SLP licensing fees.

mainEntity.name: Is Nevada part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
Does Nevada allow telepractice for SLPs?
Does Nevada have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers speech-language pathologist practice in Nevada. To work in Nevada, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Nevada?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 weeks.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Nevada while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
